question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 12.00% on 2026-04-10 for the Metaculus question "Will a federal judge be impeached by Republican Representatives in a partisan vote before January 3, 2027?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP on a federal judge impeachment question will be strictly above 12.00% on April 10. The CP is currently exactly at 12.00% (as of March 29), right at the threshold. The CP trend is upward (9%→10%→12% over 5 weeks), and political rhetoric continues (Speaker Johnson backs impeachment, Senate hearings held). However, no concrete federal legislative action has occurred — impeachment articles remain in committee since March 2025, and 40+ House Republicans opposed a related measure. Monte Carlo simulation using observed weekly volatility (SD 1.14pp) with half the observed drift gives ~63% probability of exceeding 12%, while zero drift gives ~50%. Blending these scenarios and adjusting for small sample size in the trend estimate, I arrive at 55% — slightly favoring YES due to the upward momentum but acknowledging genuine uncertainty since the CP is precisely at the boundary and the status quo resolves NO.

factors.description: Upward CP trend: CP moved from 9-10% to 12% over recent weeks, showing consistent upward momentum
Current CP is exactly at the threshold (12.00%): status quo resolves NO since threshold is strictly above
Political environment supports continued attention: Speaker Johnson backed impeachment (Jan 2026), Senate hearings held, White House supports effort
No concrete federal legislative action: impeachment articles still in committee since March 2025, no hearings scheduled, no floor vote committed, 40+ Republicans voted against related measure
Random walk model: with 1.14pp weekly SD and 1.5 weeks ahead, even zero drift gives ~50% probability of crossing above 12%
Large forecaster base (486) provides stability but recent 2pp weekly jump shows CP can move meaningfully
Kentucky state judge impeachment (March 20) may have increased salience of judicial impeachment topic, potentially nudging some forecasters upward on the federal question

reasoning:
My forecast: **55% probability** that the CP will be above 12.00% on April 10.

**Key reasoning:**
- The CP is exactly at the 12.00% threshold, making this a knife-edge call
- The upward trend (9%→10%→12% over recent weeks) provides mild evidence for YES
- Political rhetoric continues (Johnson, Cruz, White House all supporting judicial impeachment) keeping the topic salient
- But no concrete federal action has occurred — articles remain in committee, no floor vote committed, and the House GOP margin is razor-thin
- Monte Carlo modeling: zero-drift gives 50%, half-drift gives 63%, full-drift gives 74%. Blending conservatively yields ~55%
- The "strictly above" requirement means if CP stays exactly at 12.00%, it resolves NO

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/36348) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-10 19:09:37 is higher than 12.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 12.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-10 19:09:37, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
